title: Part 1a: Bulk Select & Delete across all portal sections

requirements.text: Add a checkbox to each record row in the Memberships, Publishing Works, Releases, Recording Projects, and Artist Development list pages to allow individual record selection.

quotes: Checkbox selection on each record row across all portal sections

acceptanceCriteria: Each row in MembershipsPage, PublishingWorksPage, ReleasesPage, RecordingProjectsPage, and ArtistDevelopmentPage has a checkbox on the left side.
Checking a row's checkbox marks it as selected with a visible highlight.
Unchecking removes the selection highlight.

requirements.text: Add a 'Select All' checkbox in the table header of each portal list page that toggles selection of all currently visible records. If all records are selected, it deselects all; if any are unselected, it selects all.

quotes: "Select All" toggle at the top of each table

acceptanceCriteria: A 'Select All' checkbox appears in the header row of each portal list table.
Clicking it selects all visible rows.
Clicking it again (when all are selected) deselects all rows.
The checkbox shows an indeterminate state when some but not all rows are selected.

requirements.text: Display a 'Delete Selected' bulk action button above each portal section table whenever one or more records are selected. The button must be hidden when no records are selected.

quotes: Bulk delete button that removes all selected records at once

acceptanceCriteria: The 'Delete Selected' button is not visible when no checkboxes are checked.
The button appears and shows the count of selected records (e.g., 'Delete 3 Selected') when at least one record is checked.
The button is styled as a destructive action (red/danger color).

requirements.text: Show a confirmation dialog when the 'Delete Selected' button is clicked. The dialog must display the number of records to be deleted and require explicit user confirmation before proceeding. Cancelling the dialog must leave all records intact.
acceptanceCriteria: A modal confirmation dialog appears after clicking 'Delete Selected'.
The dialog states clearly how many records will be permanently deleted.
The dialog has a 'Confirm Delete' button and a 'Cancel' button.
Clicking 'Cancel' or dismissing the dialog performs no deletion.
Clicking 'Confirm Delete' triggers the deletion flow.

requirements.text: Implement bulk delete backend logic: add a batch delete function for each entity type (Membership, PublishingWork, Release, RecordingProject, ArtistDevelopment) that accepts an array of record IDs and deletes all matching records in a single call, respecting existing authorization rules (admin can delete any, owner can delete their own).

quotes: Bulk delete button that removes all selected records at once

acceptanceCriteria: A `bulkDeleteMemberships(ids: [Text]) : async [Text]` (or equivalent) function exists in the main actor and deletes all records whose IDs are in the provided list.
Equivalent bulk delete functions exist for PublishingWork, Release, RecordingProject, and ArtistDevelopment.
Only records the caller is authorized to delete are removed; unauthorized IDs are skipped and returned as a list of failed IDs.
The function returns successfully even if some IDs are not found.

requirements.text: Wire the frontend confirmation dialog's 'Confirm Delete' action to call the appropriate backend bulk delete function for the current portal section, then refresh the list and clear all selections on success. Show a success toast with the count of deleted records. Show an error banner if the operation fails.

quotes: Bulk delete button that removes all selected records at once

acceptanceCriteria: After confirming deletion, the selected records are removed from the list without a full page reload.
A success toast notification appears confirming the number of records deleted.
All checkboxes are cleared and the 'Delete Selected' button hides after successful deletion.
If the backend call fails, an error banner is displayed and no records are removed from the UI.
The React Query cache for the affected section is invalidated so the list reflects the latest backend state.

constraints: Do not modify any files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
All Motoko backend logic must remain in the single actor in backend/main.mo.
Bulk delete must respect existing authorization rules — admins can delete any record, non-admins can only delete records they own.
nonGoals: Record history / audit log (covered in Part 1c).
